sections.title: 3.2.S.1 GENERAL INFORMATION
sections.llm_prompt: Write Section 3.2.S.1 General Information for drug substance {product_name}. Include subsections: 3.2.S.1.1 Nomenclature (INN, USAN, chemical name, CAS number, company code); 3.2.S.1.2 Structure (for biologics: amino acid sequence, disulfide bonds, glycosylation, molecular formula, molecular mass; for cell therapy: cell type, source, modifications); 3.2.S.1.3 General Properties (physical description, solubility, stability summary, pKa, other physicochemical properties).

sections.title: 3.2.S.2 MANUFACTURE
sections.llm_prompt: Write Section 3.2.S.2 Manufacture for drug substance {product_name}. Include subsections: 3.2.S.2.1 Manufacturer(s) - name, address, facility (including contract manufacturers), responsibility; 3.2.S.2.2 Description of Manufacturing Process and Process Controls - narrative description, flow diagram reference, batch size range, critical process parameters; 3.2.S.2.3 Control of Materials - raw materials, starting materials, cell banks (MCB/WCB for biologics), reference standards, animal-derived materials (TSE/BSE); 3.2.S.2.4 Controls of Critical Steps and Intermediates; 3.2.S.2.5 Process Validation and/or Evaluation (appropriate to phase); 3.2.S.2.6 Manufacturing Process Development.

sections.title: 3.2.S.2.2 Manufacturing Process Flow
sections.llm_prompt: Generate a manufacturing process flow for drug substance {product_name} ({process_type}). List each unit operation in order with its critical process parameters (temperature, pH, duration, concentration, flow rate, agitation) and in-process controls (IPCs) with acceptance criteria. For cell therapy include: thaw, activation, transduction if applicable, expansion, harvest, formulation, cryopreservation.

sections.title: 3.2.S.3 CHARACTERISATION
sections.llm_prompt: Write Section 3.2.S.3 Characterisation for drug substance {product_name}. Include subsections: 3.2.S.3.1 Elucidation of Structure and Other Characteristics (primary/secondary/tertiary/quaternary structure for biologics, potency characterization, cell identity and functional characterization for cell therapy, post-translational modifications); 3.2.S.3.2 Impurities (process-related impurities, product-related impurities/variants, residual materials, residual host cell proteins/DNA).

sections.title: 3.2.S.4 CONTROL OF DRUG SUBSTANCE
sections.llm_prompt: Generate the drug substance specification table for {product_name}. Columns: Test | Method | Acceptance Criteria. Include tests for: Identity, Appearance, pH, Osmolality, Concentration/Content, Potency/Bioactivity, Purity (SEC-HPLC, SDS-PAGE, capillary electrophoresis), Impurities (process-related, product-related), Sterility, Mycoplasma, Endotoxin, Adventitious Agents (viral for biologics), Cell count and viability (for cell therapy).

sections.title: 3.2.S.4.2 Analytical Procedures
sections.llm_prompt: Summarize each analytical procedure for the drug substance {product_name} specification. For each method provide: principle, equipment, reagents, sample preparation, system suitability, and performance characteristics. Reference method validation reports in 3.2.S.4.3.

sections.title: 3.2.S.4.3 Validation of Analytical Procedures
sections.llm_prompt: Summarize validation status of each analytical method per ICH Q2(R2). For compendial methods describe verification; for non-compendial describe validation parameters: specificity, linearity, range, accuracy, precision (repeatability, intermediate precision), LOD, LOQ, robustness. Match validation scope to phase of development.

sections.title: 3.2.S.4.4 Batch Analyses
sections.llm_prompt: Generate a batch analysis summary table for drug substance {product_name}. Columns: Batch Number | Manufacturing Date | Scale | Use (clinical/nonclinical/stability) | Key Results (potency, purity, sterility, endotoxin).

sections.title: 3.2.S.5 REFERENCE STANDARDS OR MATERIALS
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the reference standards or materials used for {product_name}. Include: source, qualification of primary reference standard (against international standard if available), working reference standard qualification, storage, stability monitoring, replacement strategy.

sections.title: 3.2.S.6 CONTAINER CLOSURE SYSTEM
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the container closure system for drug substance {product_name}. Include: container type and material (bags, vials, cryobags for cell therapy), closure materials, specifications, suitability (protection, compatibility, safety, performance), extractables/leachables considerations. Reference USP <661>, USP <1664>, and ISO 10993 if applicable.

sections.title: 3.2.S.7 STABILITY
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the stability program for drug substance {product_name} per ICH Q5C (biologics) or ICH Q1A(R2) (small molecules). Include: 3.2.S.7.1 Stability Summary and Conclusions (retest period, shelf life, recommended storage); 3.2.S.7.2 Post-approval Stability Protocol and Commitment; 3.2.S.7.3 Stability Data - conditions tested (long-term, accelerated, stressed), time points, tests performed, results tables. For cell therapy typically ultra-low temperature storage.

sections.title: REFERENCES
sections.llm_prompt: Generate a references list for the CMC drug substance module. Include: ICH Q1A(R2), ICH Q2(R2), ICH Q5A-Q5E, ICH Q6B, ICH Q7, ICH Q8(R2), ICH Q11, 21 CFR 211, 21 CFR 610, 21 CFR 1271, USP/NF, Ph.Eur., FDA CBER guidances for cell and gene therapy.

metadata.regulatory_framework: ICH M4Q CTD, ICH Q5A-E, ICH Q6B, ICH Q7, ICH Q11, 21 CFR 211, 21 CFR 610, 21 CFR 1271
